title: GraphQL Query Depth Violations
spl:
index=application sourcetype="graphql:server"
| search "depth limit" OR "complexity" OR "Query is too deep"
| stats count by operationName, client_name, depth
| where count > 10
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
description: Depth/complexity limit errors from Apollo/GraphQL server logs prevent DoS via deep queries.
value: Depth/complexity limit errors from Apollo/GraphQL server logs prevent DoS via deep queries.
grandmaExplanation: Depth/complexity limit errors from Apollo/GraphQL server logs prevent DoS using deep queries ‚Äî so broken requests, slow pages, and errors get caught before customers complain.
implementation: Log structured rejection reason. Alert on high rejection rate from single client or operation. Tune limits for legitimate mobile apps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Application logs, GraphQL gateway.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `graphql:request` `depth`, `errors`, `operationName`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Log structured rejection reason. Alert on high rejection rate from single client or operation. Tune limits for legitimate mobile apps.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=application sourcetype="graphql:server"
| search "depth limit" OR "complexity" OR "Query is too deep"
| stats count by operationName, client_name, depth
| where count > 10
```

Understanding this SPL

**GraphQL Query Depth Violations** ‚Äî Depth/complexity limit errors from Apollo/GraphQL server logs prevent DoS via deep queries.

Documented **Data sources**: `graphql:request` `depth`, `errors`, `operationName`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Application logs, GraphQL gateway. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: application; **sourcetype**: graphql:server. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=application, sourcetype="graphql:server". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by operationName, client_name, depth** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count > 10` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**GraphQL Query Depth Violations** ‚Äî Depth/complexity limit errors from Apollo/GraphQL server logs prevent DoS via deep queries.

Documented **Data sources**: `graphql:request` `depth`, `errors`, `operationName`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Application logs, GraphQL gateway. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (operation, depth, count), Bar chart (rejections by client), Line chart (depth violations over time).
visualization: Table (operation, depth, count), Bar chart (rejections by client), Line chart (depth violations over time).
